summary: Conceptual demonstration of consensus panic vulnerability. Byzantine validators (2/3+ voting power) can force honest nodes to panic and crash by proposing invalid blocks. Full PoC requires validator network simulation.
expected_security_impact: Byzantine validators with 2/3+ voting power can crash all honest nodes by proposing invalid blocks.
actual_observed_result: Code review confirms panic statements in consensus/state.go. Conceptual PoC demonstrates attack scenario and severity. Full PoC would require validator network simulation.
verification_method: Code review confirms panic statements exist at the specified locations. The conceptual PoC explains the BFT violation and impact.
evidence_produced_by_run: Panic statements identified at consensus/state.go:1532-1533 and 1719-1721
Attack scenario documented for Byzantine validator coalition
BFT safety violation explained (2/3+ Byzantine can crash honest nodes)
limitations_or_blockers: Full PoC requires simulated validator network with Byzantine behavior
Requires 2/3+ voting power which is high threshold for coalition
Complex setup involving multiple validators coordinating invalid block proposals

reproduction.source_acquisition: Conceptual demonstration based on code review of CometBFT repository
